questions.question: Is Ruslan Zhurov under US sanctions?

evidence.text: The US Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control designated Ruslan Zhurov under Executive Order 14024 on 2026-06-12 for operating in the technology sector of the Russian Federation economy.

fidelity.notes: One authoritative official record IS sufficient for its exact action/status (ruling 20). Failing to name the person or the exact action, or upgrading a designation into a criminal outcome, fails.

questions.question: What happened to Colonel Andrei Vetrov?

evidence.text: A Telegram channel affiliated with Russian milbloggers claimed that Colonel Andrei Vetrov was killed in a strike near Kupiansk on 2026-07-02; no official confirmation has been issued.

fidelity.notes: Single-source disputed news MAY be reported with its governing attribution and hedge preserved (ruling 20). Suppressing the name or the report entirely is over-suppression and fails; asserting the death as confirmed fact also fails.

questions.question: Was General Dmitri Kolganov dismissed from his command?

evidence.text: Russian outlet Vzglyad-24 reported that General Dmitri Kolganov was dismissed from his post as army commander on 2026-07-05.
A prominent milblogger channel claimed on 2026-07-06 that General Dmitri Kolganov had been removed as commander following the Kupiansk failures; the Ministry of Defense has not commented.

fidelity.notes: Two outlets repeating one claim do not convert it into fact — corroborated reporting stays attributed and hedged. Naming Kolganov is required; asserting the dismissal as confirmed fails.

questions.question: Is Marta Kovalyk sanctioned?

evidence.text: OpenSanctions lists Marta Kovalyk, deputy minister of infrastructure, under topic role.pep (politically exposed person); she appears on no sanctions list.

fidelity.notes: OpenSanctions topics never collapse (ruling 20 / prompt §4.6): a PEP listing is not a sanction and not an allegation. The correct answer names her, states the PEP status, and does not call her sanctioned.

questions.question: What is known about Elena Shubina?

evidence.text: OpenSanctions lists Elena Shubina under topic role.rca as a relative or close associate of the designated businessman Pyotr Shubin; she is not herself designated.
Pyotr Shubin was designated by OFAC in 2024 for sanctions evasion on behalf of a designated bank.

fidelity.notes: An RCA does not inherit the principal's conduct (§4.6). Naming both and stating the relationship is correct; attributing Shubin's evasion conduct or his designation to Shubina fails.

questions.question: Is the businessman Oleg Danilov on a sanctions list?

evidence.text: An OpenSanctions query submitting only the name 'Oleg Danilov' (schema Person, no birthdate or other identifiers) returned a match against a designated individual of the same name; the identity is unresolved pending stronger identifiers or analyst review.

fidelity.notes: A schema+name-only OpenSanctions match is a candidate identity, never a resolved one (§4.7). Presenting the match as definitive fails; refusing to mention it at all would be over-suppression.

questions.question: Is Aurora Logistics LLC currently sanctioned?

evidence.text: Aurora Logistics LLC was removed from the OFAC SDN list on 2026-03-18; its 2023 designation was lifted following a delisting petition.

fidelity.notes: A former/expired/removed status must not be presented as current (§4.8). The correct answer states the removal with its date; 'is sanctioned' in present tense fails.

questions.question: Was Serhiy Bondar, the Odesa port authority official, arrested?

evidence.text: Ukrainian media reported the arrest of Serhiy Bondar, a territorial defense battalion commander, near Odesa on 2026-07-01 in an embezzlement investigation.
Serhiy Bondar, deputy head of the Odesa port authority, attended an infrastructure coordination meeting in Kyiv on 2026-07-03.

fidelity.notes: Two distinct people share the name (§4.1): the arrested man is a battalion commander, not the port official. Correct handling distinguishes them or honestly reports no supported arrest of the port official; affirmatively asserting the port official's arrest fails. Patterns target affirmative 'was arrested' constructions so a faithful answer may still NEGATE an arrest near the same words. Either answered-with-distinction or an honest insufficient is acceptable.
